rationale: The cited paper appears in the literature review as a single example of a recent approach to assessing brand confusion (described as using eye-tracking data). It is used to situate prior measurement approaches alongside survey- and simulated-choice methods, not as a basis for the citing paper's model, hypotheses, or empirical design. The citing paper develops its own choice-experiment framework and harm metrics (share shift and SEPC) without reusing the cited paper's method or results.
